records.ety: [L. inexorabilis: cf. F. inexorable. See In- not, and Exorable, Adore.]

defs: Not to be persuaded or moved by entreaty or prayer; firm; determined; unyielding; unchangeable; inflexible; relentless; -- of people and impersonal forces; as, an inexorable prince or tyrant; an inexorable judge; the inexorable advance of a glacier.
quotes: You are more inhuman, more inexorable, O, ten times more than tigers of Hyrcania.
